title: Vasquez to Lead Rare Disease Unit After Oryza Takeover
body:
Dr. Lena Vasquez, who co-founded Oryza Therapeutics six years ago, will stay on to run rare disease research at the acquiring company, Halcyon Genomics, following the $1.8 billion purchase announced earlier this week.

Vasquez built Oryza from a three-person lab at UC San Diego into a 140-person operation with a pipeline of four CRISPR-based programs. The flagship candidate targets a disorder of branched-chain amino acid metabolism that affects infants and, if untreated, can cause severe neurological damage.

"The infrastructure Halcyon brings — manufacturing, regulatory, global clinical networks — is something we simply couldn't have built in the timeframe patients need," Vasquez told this publication in a phone interview Wednesday.

Halcyon's CEO, Ferrara, has signaled the company intends to accelerate Oryza's lead program toward an IND filing within 18 months. The two companies have worked together informally since 2023, when Halcyon participated as a limited partner in Oryza's Series C.

The deal remains subject to Hart-Scott-Rodino review. Several analysts said they do not expect antitrust complications given the niche focus of Oryza's portfolio. Closing is anticipated by late June.
